title: Tutorial: Team Telegram Assistant
summary: Complete walkthrough for setting up a Telegram bot powered by Hermes Agent for multiple team members, covering BotFather setup, gateway configuration, team access via static allowlists or DM pairing, scheduled tasks, and production hardening.

env_vars.name: TELEGRAM_HOME_CHANNEL
env_vars.purpose: Telegram channel ID for cron delivery

env_vars.name: TERMINAL_BACKEND
env_vars.purpose: Set to docker for container isolation

cli_commands.purpose: Approve a team member's pairing request
List all pending and approved users

config_keys.description: Tool activity visibility: off | new | all | verbose

slash_commands.purpose: Set the home channel for cron delivery

code_examples.caption: Linux service management
code_examples.code:
hermes gateway start
hermes gateway stop
journalctl --user -u hermes-gateway -f
sudo loginctl enable-linger $USER

code_examples.caption: Docker terminal backend config
code_examples.code:
terminal:
  backend: docker
  container_cpu: 1
  container_memory: 5120
  container_persistent: true

gotchas: Never set GATEWAY_ALLOW_ALL_USERS=true on a bot with terminal access
Telegram user IDs are permanent numbers (like 123456789), NOT @usernames which can change
Pairing codes expire after 1 hour; after 5 failed approval attempts, platform enters 1-hour lockout
On macOS, launchd plist captures PATH at install time — re-run hermes gateway install after installing new tools
Cron job prompts must be completely self-contained — no memory of prior conversations
full_text:
# Set Up a Team Telegram Assistant

This tutorial walks you through setting up a Telegram bot powered by Hermes Agent that multiple team members can use.
